data.title: Mosaic upgraded at RBC Capital on strength in phosphate market
data.description:
The Mosaic Co. (NYSE:MOS) late Wednesday was upgraded to Outperform from a previous investment rating of Sector Perform by analysts at RBC Capital Markets. They said tightening phosphate markets and operational tailwinds position the fertilizer giant for renewed earnings momentum.

In a May 7 note to clients, RBC analyst Andrew Wong said Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) is trading at a discount relative to its growth potential, noting the stock currently sits at just 4 to 4.5 times forward 12-month spot earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. He said he expects tightening global supply and steady demand to continue supporting strong phosphate pricing, while Mosaic (MOS) benefits from several upcoming catalysts.

Phosphate supply remains constrained

“We expect phosphate markets to remain tight given steady demand growth and limited supply,” Wong wrote.

He highlighted limited global supply additions outside of modest increases from Saudi Arabia and Morocco, offset by ongoing restrictions on Chinese exports aimed at securing domestic supply.

In the U.S., Mosaic (MOS) stands to benefit further from trade protections. Tariffs and countervailing duties are keeping the U.S. market especially tight, Wong said, prompting RBC to raise its 2025 and 2026 price forecasts for diammonium phosphate at the NOLA hub by approximately 5%.

Potash markets also resilient

While Wong sees some potential softening in potash prices in the second half of 2025 due to easing North American demand and improved supply, he believes the downside is limited. Strong global demand, particularly from China where inventories remain low, should support prices in the $325–$350 per tonne range, he said.

Operational support on the horizon

RBC expects Mosaic’s (MOS) operations to show notable improvement over the next six to 12 months. These include higher phosphate output, lower per-tonne costs as production scales, and increased distribution volumes in Brazil with the ramp-up of a new blending facility in the second half of the year.

“Management has set out clear plans for operational improvements that if executed well should meaningfully benefit both margins and sales volumes,” Wong said.

The Esterhazy mine is also expected to contribute more low-cost potash production, reducing the company’s cost base over time.

Free cash flow and strategic monetization

Mosaic’s (MOS) capital efficiency is expected to improve as capex declines and pricing remains robust. RBC forecasts $900 million in free cash flow for 2025 and $1.2 billion for 2026 -- representing yields of 9% and 12%, respectively.

Wong also sees potential for further value creation through asset sales.

“We anticipate continued monetization of non-core assets, including idle phosphate facilities in Brazil, the Carlsbad potash mine, and possible early partial divestment” of Mosaic’s (MOS) stake in Ma’aden, he wrote.

RBC raised its 2025 and 2026 ebitda estimates to $2.9 billion and $2.8 billion, respectively, up from previous estimates of $2.6 billion and $2.4 billion.

The bank raised its price target on Mosaic (MOS) shares to $40 a share from $30 a share previously, based on a sum-of-the-parts analysis and enterprise value-to-ebitda ratios.

data.title: Groupon raises billings growth target to 3-5% for FY 2025 with enhanced focus on top cities
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Groupon, Inc. (GRPN) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Dusan Senkypl highlighted that Q1 results exceeded guidance on billings, adjusted EBITDA, and showed a slight beat on revenue. The global billings grew by 1.4% year-over-year, with North America Local accelerating to 11% growth, marking the first double-digit growth in this segment since 2017.
  • The company’s "Things to Do" vertical reported double-digit billings growth for the fifth consecutive quarter, significantly outperforming industry trends.
  • The international local business, excluding Italy, achieved a 5% year-over-year increase in billings, driven by strong performance in key markets like Spain, Germany, the UK, and France.
  • Groupon announced multiple leadership changes, including the appointments of Josef Buryan as Chief Marketing Officer, Ales Drabek as Chief Technology Officer, Barbara Weisz as Chief Revenue Officer, Filip Popovic as Chief Commercial Officer, and Marie Havlickova as Chief Product Officer.
  • CFO Jiri Ponrt noted that the company completed the sale of its Giftcloud subsidiary, removing $6 million in revenue and $4 million in adjusted EBITDA from future consolidated results while maintaining revenue and EBITDA guidance for 2025.

Outlook

  • Groupon raised its 2025 billings growth guidance from 2-4% to 3-5%, citing confidence in its core business performance.
  • Management reiterated that the focus remains on improving revenue and adjusted EBITDA while maintaining stability in the core business despite external challenges.
  • The company expects further acceleration in year-over-year growth for billings and revenue in Q2 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 metrics included growth in gross billings and adjusted EBITDA exceeding previous guidance. Revenue slightly surpassed expectations, though specific figures were not disclosed.
  • North America Local delivered 11% year-over-year billings growth, showcasing the success of Groupon’s hyper-local strategy.
  • Increased focus on higher-quality merchants led to a 43% year-over-year rise in the number of North American merchants generating over $1 million in billings.

Q&A

  • Bobby Brooks, Northland Capital: Asked about the increase in merchants exceeding $1 million in billings. CEO Senkypl credited the hyper-geo approach, focusing on inventory quality and deal performance.
  • Eric Sheridan, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about macroeconomic trends and their impact on merchant pipeline. CEO Senkypl stated that the current environment is a tailwind, enabling deeper partnerships with enterprise and local clients.
  • Sean McGowan, ROTH Capital: Queried about international market updates. CEO Senkypl highlighted Spain’s strong double-digit growth and positive trends in Germany, UK, and France.
  • Analysts also explored the impact of AI integration, with CEO Senkypl elaborating on plans to enhance sales processes, deal optimization, and compatibility with AI-driven search engines.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on operational improvements and macroeconomic tailwinds, but raised detailed questions on marketing ROI and international expansion timelines.
  • Management displayed confidence in its strategic initiatives, emphasizing progress in platform modernization and deal quality. CEO Senkypl struck a positive tone, highlighting significant improvements across key markets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Billings growth accelerated in Q1 2025 compared to Q4 2024, particularly in North America Local.
  • Guidance for billings growth increased from 2-4% to 3-5%, reflecting stronger-than-expected performance.
  • Leadership changes and international market strategies showed continuity, with Spain maintaining significant growth momentum.
  • Management’s tone was more assertive this quarter, reflecting confidence in the company’s transformation efforts and growth potential.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged deliberate compression in take rates as a short-term revenue headwind but stressed its importance for long-term marketplace health.
  • Analysts questioned the scalability of Groupon’s hyper-local and category-specific strategies across all markets.
  • Concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and their impact on consumer spending and merchant activity were addressed cautiously.

Final Takeaway

Groupon delivered a strong start to 2025, exceeding guidance on key metrics and raising its billings growth target for the year. The company’s focus on quality over quantity, supported by leadership changes and strategic initiatives, is driving growth in key markets. With ongoing platform enhancements and AI integration, Groupon aims to strengthen its position as a leader in local experiences, signaling confidence in long-term sustainable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Peloton outlines FY2026 strategy amid Q3 profitability improvements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Peloton Interactive (PTON) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Peter Stern highlighted achieving key metrics at the high end of or above guidance for Q3 2025. The company reported slight growth in Paid Connected Fitness subscriptions, improved unit economics, significant positive adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow. Stern emphasized the strategic objectives of improving member outcomes, expanding market reach, and enhancing operational efficiency.
  • The company introduced Personalized Plans in January, with nearly 500,000 members starting a plan by the end of Q3. Engagement metrics indicated higher participation and use of various fitness disciplines.
  • New retail strategies were noted, including a micro-store test in Nashville and collaborations with Amazon for seasonal sales, which showed promising results in driving hardware sales.
  • CFO Liz Coddington stated, "We are pleased with our third quarter results as we delivered at the high end of or exceeded guidance on key metrics and continued to make meaningful progress on improving unit economics and profitability."

Outlook

  • Management increased the full-year FY 2025 guidance for Paid Connected Fitness subscriptions to a range of 2.77 million to 2.79 million, and for total revenue to $2.455 billion to $2.47 billion. Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to $330 million to $350 million.
  • The company outlined plans to expand international programming by leveraging AI for class translations, indicating readiness for scaling international markets in the future.

Financial Results

  • Q3 revenue was $624 million, with $205 million from Connected Fitness products and $419 million from subscriptions. Total revenue exceeded the midpoint of guidance by $9 million.
  • Gross margins improved to 51%, with Connected Fitness products gross margin rising to 14.3% and subscription gross margin at 69%. The improvements were attributed to cost restructuring and favorable revenue mix.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 was $89 million, exceeding guidance, and free cash flow was $95 million. The company ended the quarter with $914 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents.
  • Operating expenses decreased 23% year-over-year, reflecting progress in cost reduction strategies.

Q&A

  • Youssef Squali, Truist, asked about progress on key initiatives and management changes. Stern emphasized cost reductions, unit economic improvements, and a focus on innovation in hardware and software as priorities for returning to growth.
  • Arpine Kocharyan, UBS, inquired about macroeconomic impacts and subscription pricing strategy. CFO Coddington noted overall resilience in the subscription business and stated that pricing adjustments are under consideration, particularly for hardware and international markets.
  • Simeon Siegel, BMO, queried about member engagement and activation fees. Stern highlighted consistent low churn and strong member satisfaction, while Coddington elaborated on the positive impact of activation fees on revenue and member retention.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed cautious optimism, focusing on the company's ability to sustain profitability and manage churn while strategizing for growth.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing fiscal discipline and strategic initiatives for long-term growth, with Stern stating, "We are starting to be able to acquire new members much more cost effectively."

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 showed notable improvements in profitability metrics compared to Q2 2025. Adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow continued to grow, supported by cost reductions and improved gross margins.
  • Subscription churn improved to 1.2% in Q3 from 1.4% in Q2, and net Paid Connected Fitness subscriptions saw slight growth quarter-over-quarter.
  • Management’s tone remained focused on operational excellence and growth strategies, building on the progress reported in the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainties potentially impacting hardware sales, though mitigated by pricing strategies and financing options.
  • Analysts flagged concerns about maintaining engagement levels and addressing secondary market subscription dynamics.

Final Takeaway

Peloton delivered a strong Q3 2025 performance, surpassing guidance on key metrics and showcasing effective cost discipline and strategic growth initiatives. Management emphasized its focus on innovation and market expansion while maintaining profitability. With raised FY 2025 guidance and a clear strategy for FY 2026, the company aims to solidify its position in the connected fitness market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Vertical Aerospace expands its collaboration with Honeywell as it looks for eVTOL breakthroughs
data.description:
Vertical Aerospace (NYSE:EVTL) and Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) announced a new long-term agreement on Thursday to deepen their partnership and accelerate the certification and production of Vertical Aerospace's (NYSE:EVTL) VX4 electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft. 

The expanded collaboration focuses on certifying critical systems for the VX4, including the aircraft management system and the flight controls system, featuring Honeywell’s compact fly-by-wire technology. Those systems are designed to enhance safety, automation, and ease of flight, with the Anthem flight deck serving as the digital core of the aircraft and the fly-by-wire system providing precise electronic control. Both systems will be certified to a stringent safety standard set by the UK Civil Aviation Authority, in coordination with the European Union Aviation Safety Agency, aiming for a catastrophic failure rate of less than one in a billion flight hours-matching commercial airliner standards and surpassing many competitors.

The EVTL-HON partnership, which is valued at up to $1 billion over the next decade based on projected contract value, is seen leveraging shared expertise and resources to expedite development, with Vertical targeting VX4 certification in 2028 and at least 150 aircraft deliveries by 2030. The companies are also integrating newly designed Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) inceptors for improved pilot control.

Looking ahead, Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) plans to advance the VX4’s flight test program, moving into the wingborne flight phase-a crucial step toward full certification. The company plans to continue system and component testing, develop an additional full-scale prototype, and work closely with regulators to expand its Permit to Fly.

data.title: CTP N.V. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• CTP N.V. press release (OTCPK:CTPVF): Q1 Rental Income of €182.5 million, up 15.9% y-o-y, and like-for-like y-o-y rental growth of 4.2%.
  • EPRA NTA per share increased by 2.8% in Q1 to €18.58 and 12.6% y-o-y.
  • Company specific adjusted EPRA earnings increased by 12.9% y-o-y to €98.7 million. CTP’s Company-specific adjusted EPRA EPS amounted to €0.21, an increase of 6.9%, on track to reach the guidance. 
  • The Group confirms its €0.86 – €0.88 Company-specific adjusted EPRA EPS guidance for 2025, which represents a 8 – 10% growth compared to 2024.

data.title: Peloton's sales erosion and bottom-line miss overshadows raised guidance
data.description:
Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) shares are losing ground on Thursday as a significant miss in fiscal third quarter earnings and a 13% drop in sales overshadowed results that exceeded the company’s expectations, as well as an improved outlook for the current fiscal year.

“In Q3, we delivered at the high end of or exceeded guidance on our key financial metrics while also continuing to execute on our FY25 operating goals. We delivered our fifth consecutive quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow due to improving hardware unit economics and right-sizing our overall cost structure,” Peloton CEO Peter Stern said in a letter to shareholders.

Unfortunately, the metrics failed to impress investors who were unloading shares at the open, resulting in a loss of 12% and a twelve-day low for PTON.

Led by a 27% drop in connected fitness revenue and a 4% decline in subscription revenue, Peloton’s (NASDAQ:PTON) total sales were down 13% from the same quarter a year ago, but $3M above Wall Street’s expectations.

But while revenue was down, cost-cutting in advertising and marketing, R&D, and fewer restructuring expenses improved profitability by 73%, resulting in a loss of $0.12 per share, but still 6 cents worse than expected.

Looking ahead to the fiscal full year, Peloton (NASDAQ:PTON) raised its revenue guidance by $7.5B at the midpoint to be within a range of $2.455B to $2.470B, versus the consensus estimate of $2.46B.

Total gross margin outlook was left unchanged at 50% and incorporates expectation for minimal impacts from tariffs on profits.

Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised by $15M at the midpoint to $330M to $350M, reflecting improvements in profitability and operating expense savings.

To reflect a $5M headwind from tariffs on free cash flow in the fourth quarter, the company expects FCF to be ~$250M.

As the company continues to improve operations and increase membership, Stern assured shareholders that “during this period of economic uncertainty, we believe Peloton is well-positioned to maintain its leadership within the global fitness and wellness industry. Our net churn rate reflects the continued resilience of our Connected Fitness subscription base and the value that our members place on Peloton as an important part of their ongoing fitness and wellness journey.”

data.title: Sen. Ted Cruz: Leaving tariffs permanently would be an ‘utter disaster’ for economy
data.description:
“If you leave tariffs in forever, they’re just taxes on the American people,” said Republican Senator Ted Cruz of Texas.

In an interview with CNBC, Senator Ted Cruz advocated for using tariffs as a negotiating tool rather than permanent policy, stating that President Donald Trump has an “historic opportunity” to leverage existing tariffs to secure lower trade barriers with global partners.

Cruz emphasized that the administration should aim to “achieve dramatically lower tariffs from other countries in exchange” for current U.S. tariffs. He noted that over 30 nations have reportedly shown interest in negotiating reduced tariffs and market access.

The Texas senator, who is also the commerce committee chairman, expressed strong support for the negotiation approach, claiming it would create an “historic economic win for jobs and American business and productivity that we’ve never seen before.”

He framed this strategy as particularly beneficial for American workers and economic growth.

However, Cruz explicitly warned against maintaining high tariffs permanently, describing such an approach as an “utter disaster” for the economy.

“Tariffs are taxes,” he emphasized. “If you leave tariffs in forever, they’re just taxes on the American people.”

According to Cruz, he regularly urges the president to use tariffs as leverage for securing better global trade terms. He mentioned that other prominent figures, including Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Elon Musk, also advocate for this negotiation-focused approach to trade policy.

The senator concluded with optimism about the administration potentially pursuing a negotiation strategy, suggesting there would be “rich irony” if Trump ultimately became “the most free trade president that this country has ever seen” through his tactical use of tariffs.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on China tariffs:

  • Trump issues 90-day pause on most reciprocal tariffs except for China
  • China calls on U.S. to lift all unilateral tariffs, denies talks
  • Trump signals willingness to ease China tariffs amid trade freeze
  • Apollo: Beyond tariffs, U.S. companies are deeply tied to China’s consumer market
  • China unveils new policy measures to counter U.S. tariff pressure
